Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/lahore-v1 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
Sometimes the user might send you a message they meant to send in a different workspace or a different chat. If something doesn't make sense in the context of your work, just ask. 
If the user asks for help with Conductor, you can ask them to go to "Help -> Send Feedback" to get in touch with our team.

content.text: Wait — let me check what CSS framework is being used to understand the correct border color class. Let me check the tailwind config.

content.content: No matches found

content.thinking:
The CSS shows `--color-border: var(--border)` and `--border: #1e1e26` for dark mode. In Tailwind v4, `border` class uses `--color-border` by default, so `border` alone should already use the theme color. But the screenshot shows a white border, which means the default `border` class isn't picking up the theme color.

Looking at the CSS: `--color-border: var(--border);` on line 23 — this is the Tailwind v4 way of setting the default border color. So `border` should already use `#1e1e26` in dark mode.


But the screenshot clearly shows a white border, so something's off. Maybe the `border` utility only sets the width and not the color by default, letting the browser default show through. Let me check what's actually happening at that section with the white border around it. I think the issue is that the `border` utility in Tailwind only sets the width and style, not the color—the color needs to come from `border-border` explicitly, which applies the `--color-border` variable. Adding `border-border` to the header and table rows should fix the white border problem.

content.text: Good — the theme has `--border: #1e1e26` for dark mode. The `border` utility sets width but the color isn't being picked up. My edit adding `border-border` should fix the header. Let me also fix the table row borders.

content.text:
All `border` usages in the file now include `border-border` for the theme color. The three fixes:

- **Line 710**: Section header border
- **Line 736**: Table header row bottom border  
- **Line 749**: Table body row bottom borders

This ensures borders use `--border: #1e1e26` (dark theme) instead of the browser's default white/light border color.

Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

content.thinking:
I see many `border-b` and `border` classes without `border-border` across multiple files. But wait - maybe the real fix should be at the CSS level instead of adding `border-border` to every element. In Tailwind v4, the default border color should be set by `--color-border`. Let me check if the issue is in how the CSS variable is defined.

Actually, looking at the CSS output earlier:
```
app/src/client/styles.css:23:  --color-border: var(--border);
```

This should make `border` use the theme color. But maybe it's not working because of how Tailwind v4 handles this. 

Let me look at the styles.css file directly to see if I can set a global default border color there instead of patching every element individually.

content.text: Rather than fixing every component individually, let me fix this globally in CSS. The issue is the default border color isn't being applied.

content.thinking:
In Tailwind v4, `--color-border` sets the color for `border-border` utility, but the plain `border` utility only sets `border-width: 1px` and `border-style: solid`. The default border color in browsers is `currentColor`, which would be the text foreground color (white/light in dark mode).

To fix this globally, I need to set the default border color. In Tailwind v4, I can use `@layer base` to set `border-color` on all elements, similar to what shadcn/ui does.

Let me check if there's already a base layer setting for this.

There's no global `@layer base` rule setting the default border color. In Tailwind v4 with shadcn, the standard approach is to add a base layer that sets `border-color: var(--border)` on all elements. This way, the `border` utility (which only adds width+style) will use the theme border color instead of `currentColor`.

Let me add this global rule. Let me first check what's around line 334 where border-color is already set to see the context.

content.text: The issue is there's no global base style setting `border-color` on all elements. In Tailwind v4, `border` only sets width — color defaults to `currentColor` (white text in dark mode). I need to add a global base rule.
